question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user send a POST to /api/v1/contracts/call with gas=Long.MAX_VALUE so that throttleManager.throttle(request) is invoked with an overflowed gas value before validateContractMaxGasLimit() rejects it, causing integer overflow in the throttle bucket and bypassing rate limiting entirely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: constructServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user set the from field to any arbitrary Ethereum address (e.g., a whale or privileged contract address) since there is no authentication of the sender identity, causing the EVM to execute calls as if originating from that spoofed address and bypassing any contract-level access controls that check msg.sender?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: constructServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user set to=null or to='' so that receiver defaults to Address.ZERO, triggering unintended contract-creation code paths or special zero-address handling in the EVM execution layer, leading to unexpected state changes or information disclosure?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: constructServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded isStaticCall=false to force all calls to be treated as non-static, bypassing any downstream static-call guards that would otherwise prevent state mutation, and causing unintended side effects in the EVM?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a data field containing a valid hex string of arbitrarily large size (e.g., megabytes) since there is no maximum length constraint on the @Hex annotation for the data field, causing excessive memory allocation in hexToBytes() and a denial-of-service condition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: validateContractMaxGasLimit()] Can an unprivileged external user submit gas=evmProperties.getMaxGasLimit() (exactly at the boundary) to pass validateContractMaxGasLimit() since the check is strictly greater-than (>), and then use the maximum allowed gas to perform the most computationally expensive possible EVM execution, amplifying CPU cost per request?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that throttleManager.restore() is only called on InvalidParametersException but NOT on other runtime exceptions (e.g., IllegalArgumentException from hexToBytes, NullPointerException, or RuntimeException from processCall) to permanently deplete throttle tokens for other users, causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: constructServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user set value=Long.MAX_VALUE (passing @PositiveOrZero) to simulate a transfer of an astronomically large amount, causing integer overflow or unexpected behavior in downstream value-handling logic within contractExecutionService.processCall()?
